message:
style(e2e): clippy nits on comprehensive_bench CliOptions + bd_ezg4p_prefetch test

Last few lints surfaced after the previous e2e clippy cleanup pass
in this same batch.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/comprehensive_bench.rs

- CliOptions: tag with #[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)].
  CliOptions carries quick, filter, plus a handful of independent
  on/off mode flags (one per top-level subcommand variant).
  Bundling them into a single ModeFlags struct or an enum would
  force the parser through one extra builder step for each flag
  for no readability win.  The allow stays scoped to this struct.

- DetectedEnvironment::print_summary: trailing semicolon on the
  `emit_line(to_stdout, ...)` call inside the `Some(cpu_cores)`
  arm of the inner match so the arm consistently terminates as a
  statement (clippy::semicolon_if_nothing_returned).

crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/bd_ezg4p_prefetch.rs

- PrefetchStatsSnapshot: tag with #[allow(clippy::struct_field_names)].
  All four fields (prefetch_issued_count, prefetch_hit_count,
  prefetch_miss_count, prefetch_evicted_count) legitimately share
  the "prefetch_" prefix because the snapshot exists specifically
  to bundle prefetch counters; renaming them to issued_count etc.
  would lose the disambiguation against the broader page cache
  counters in the same module.

- SharedPrefetchStore::read_page: tag with
  #[allow(clippy::useless_let_if_seq)].  The function builds a
  `prefetched` flag in three steps:
      let prefetched = { ... }
      if prefetched { ... } else { ... }
  Folding into a single match would force the borrow over
  self.stats to span both arms, defeating the borrow scope the
  current shape was specifically chosen for.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
